proof_strategy.principle: Prove denial at the exact layer that must deny: auth, actor mapping, tenant scope, route shell, action authority, payload projection or object scope.
proof_strategy.anti_overclaim: A route-rendering pass, a visible disabled CTA or a positive allow test is not negative access proof.

negative_proof_cases.existing_proof: tests/auth-spine.spec.ts: fails closed for missing or invalid current-user JWT

negative_proof_cases.scenario: Invalid MFA code cannot issue DB-user JWT.
negative_proof_cases.existing_proof: tests/auth-spine.spec.ts: issues a safe JWT only after MFA 123456 and resolves current-user context

negative_proof_cases.scenario: Unknown role or tenant cannot be accepted in strict demo/providerless paths.
required_assertions: createDemoSession fallback is explicitly not accepted as strict proof

negative_proof_cases.scenario: Mapped client actor cannot access a different tenant context.
negative_proof_cases.existing_proof: tests/providerless-scope.spec.ts and tests/auth-spine.spec.ts current-user bridge cross-tenant check

negative_proof_cases.scenario: Payload access with tenant-owned subject but no tenant context fails closed.
required_assertions: route-level permissiveness does not imply payload access

negative_proof_cases.scenario: Route shell can render while action authority is denied.
negative_proof_cases.existing_proof: tests/providerless-scope.spec.ts: allows route shell access while denying action authority

negative_proof_cases.scenario: Object-scoped payload targets wrong object id.
Object-scoped payload has no current object scope.
Admin/Security cannot force compliance release, evidence sufficiency, client visibility or export.
required_assertions: admin governance success remains separate from process release

negative_proof_cases.scenario: Advisor approval cannot become compliance release.
required_assertions: advisor approval allow does not imply release allow
compliance_officer release requires matching object scope

negative_proof_cases.scenario: Client principal cannot see unreleased decision/recommendation internal payload.
required_assertions: hiddenFields includes internal rationale/compliance notes/audit internals

negative_proof_cases.scenario: External/limited roles cannot create, download or share restricted export packages.

coverage_gaps_for_later_tickets.gap: Evidence lifecycle sufficiency needs end-to-end negative proof against upload/review/release flow, not only PermissionEngine unit proof.
No-client-leak projection must be proven through internal draft states and client projection route/API, not only abstract visibility projection.
Denied attempts should create or preserve audit/event proof at the persistence spine where mutation services are involved.
Export package redaction needs package-level negative proof that restricted fields never enter generated client-safe output.

bold_cleanup_recommendations.recommendation: Create a shared negative-access test table from this plan and require every new P0 process service to add at least one denial case before any positive implementation claim.
bold_cleanup_recommendations.legacy_removed: Stops positive-only demos from being treated as safety proof.

bold_cleanup_recommendations.recommendation: Ban createDemoSession fallback usage in new negative tests; require requireDemoSession or DB/JWT CurrentUserContext.
bold_cleanup_recommendations.legacy_removed: Removes silent fallback masking from security proof.

bold_cleanup_recommendations.recommendation: Make reasonCode part of acceptance, not just allowed=false.
bold_cleanup_recommendations.legacy_removed: Prevents accidental denial for the wrong reason from passing.

bold_cleanup_recommendations.recommendation: Track no-payload/no-mutation/no-release assertions separately from HTTP status.
bold_cleanup_recommendations.legacy_removed: Stops 401/403-only tests from missing payload leakage.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Negative access proof cases cover auth, actor, tenant, route/action split, object scope, admin non-bypass and client projection.
Existing proof files are identified and later gaps are not hidden.
The plan requires reasonCode and no-payload/no-mutation/no-release assertions.

overclaim_guard: This is the negative access proof plan for BD-02. It does not close BD-04, BD-06, BD-09, BD-10, BD-11 or BD-12 end-to-end proof obligations.
